quizOnboarding.title: Quiz Guide
quizOnboarding.description: Learn when to use each quiz type and how recommendations work (based on your spaced repetition progress).

intro.text: Recommendations are meant to help you pick the best next action, not to restrict you. The system looks at your question statuses and groups questions into quiz types. Then it recommends the type that gives you the biggest learning benefit right now (example priority: Wrong → Weak → Review → Learning → New).

steps.title: How “N questions with the same status” are learned (step-by-step)

items.text: The system checks your topic and counts how many questions belong to each learning bucket: Wrong, Weak, Review, Learning, New. These buckets come from your spaced repetition tracking records.

items.step: Step 2 — It picks the best focus
items.text:
If you have many questions in the same “high priority” status (for example Wrong), that type becomes a strong candidate for recommendation because it usually delivers the fastest improvement.
Example: You have 5 Wrong questions → practicing them prevents repeated mistakes.

items.step: Step 3 — It generates a quiz from that group
items.text:
When you select a quiz type (or click Start now from Recommendation), the app fetches questions matching that type’s filter (status / EF / due rules).
You get a quiz that is focused: all questions come from the same “goal”.
During the quiz, you answer a batch of questions that share the same learning intention:
list: Review: refresh questions that are due now (scheduled reviews)
Learning: stabilize new knowledge still being built

items.step: Step 5 — The system updates your status after submission
items.text: After you submit, each question’s tracking record is updated. This is where spaced repetition “moves” questions between statuses.

note.title: Typical movement (simplified)
list: If you answer correctly and confidently, the question is more likely to move toward Review with a longer interval.
If you answer incorrectly, it becomes Wrong / Relearning so you see it again sooner.
If you keep struggling with it, its difficulty stays high, so it may remain Weak (low EF) and appear more often.

items.text: The next time you open Quiz, counts update and the recommendation may change. That’s why it feels “personalized”: it follows what you actually did in the last sessions.

items.title: Wrong (highest urgency)
items.text: You recently got these wrong and they are typically tagged as relearning. Doing them now reduces repeated mistakes fast.

items.title: Weak
items.text: These are questions with a low easiness factor (you often find them hard). Practicing them improves long-term stability.

items.title: Review
items.text: These are scheduled review questions that are due now. This is the classic spaced repetition “right time” practice.

items.title: Learning
items.text: Questions still in the early learning phase. Doing them helps you move items into stable review.

items.title: New (lowest urgency)
items.text: Fresh, unseen items. Great when you want to expand coverage, but not always the best next step if you have many wrong/weak items.

notes.title: Important notes
items: Recommendation is a hint, not a rule — you can always choose any quiz type.
Counts can differ slightly between “Recommendation counts” and “Generate quiz counts” if your tracking updates between requests (for example, after finishing a quiz).
“Review” is usually the only type that must be due (based on next review time). Other types are often based on status/EF, so they can still appear even if next review time is in the future.

initial.label: Initial
initial.detail:
Includes all questions in this topic, regardless of learning history.

Use when:
  • Starting a topic for the first time
  • Practicing freely without restrictions

new.label: New
new.detail:
Questions without any spaced-repetition record.

Use when:
  • You just added new questions
  • You want first exposure before review

learning.label: Learning
learning.detail:
Questions currently in the learning phase of the SM-2 algorithm.

Use when:
  • Building memory
  • Your confidence is still unstable

review.label: Review
review.detail:
Questions scheduled for review based on SM-2 intervals.

Best for long-term retention and memory reinforcement.

weak.label: Weak
weak.detail:
Questions with a low easiness factor.

Focuses on concepts you repeatedly struggle with and helps stabilize them.

wrong.label: Wrong
wrong.detail:
Questions you answered incorrectly and are currently in the relearning state.

Ideal for fixing mistakes early and preventing repeated errors.
